content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Chain Prompting: Explicit Stepwise Fact Elicitation to Reduce Hallucination in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often hallucinate or generate factually incorrect responses, especially for multi-hop or complex questions. Existing methods insufficiently control the chain of factual dependencies within generated answers.",
  "motivation": "Improving the factual reliability of LLMs is critical for deployment in high-stakes domains such as science and healthcare. Recent work has focused on retrieval, citations, model alignment, and entropy-based detection, but explicit reasoning about factual dependencies at the prompting stage remains underexplored.",
  "method": "We propose 'Fact-Chain Prompting,' a novel prompting method that decomposes user queries into explicit fact chains. For each target question, the prompt instructs the LLM to enumerate the minimal set of atomic, verifiable facts needed to answer the question, and to justify the answer by referencing each fact in sequence. A post-generation consistency check is performed by re-prompting the model to verify the factuality of each chain element in isolation. This approach enforces stepwise factual grounding, contrasting with end-to-end generation or citation-only methods.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate Fact-Chain Prompting on open-domain QA datasets (e.g., HotpotQA, LongFact). Baselines include standard prompting, retrieval-augmented generation, and citation prompting. Metrics include factual accuracy (human and automated), hallucination rates, and chain completeness. Ablations assess the contribution of explicit chain construction and fact verification stages."
}
